title: Thais demand government action on living costs
desc: Thais say their top concern is rising living costs they are urging the new government to take swift action on the issue, according to a National Statistical Office (NSO) survey.

title: Speaker sees 'no disruptions' in PM vote
desc: House Speaker Sophon Zaram expressed confidence that Thursday's parliamentary vote to select a new prime minister will proceed smoothly, in line with regulations and fairness.

title: Cabinet focuses on illicit tour operators
desc: The cabinet has instructed the Tourism and Sports Ministry to accelerate efforts to tackle the use of nominees in tourism businesses, including so-called "zero-dollar tours", as they affect the country's tourism industry.

title: Transport fares capped in line with fuel prices
desc: The Ministry of Transport has asked operators to maintain current fares while the government continues to cap domestic fuel prices to ease the burden on the public, Transport Minister Phiphat Ratchakitprakan said on Wednesday.

title: Activist leads petition opposing non-Thai student admissions policy
desc: Activist leads petition opposing non-Thai student admissions policy Legacy A group led by activist Tae Achiwa gathered outside Thailand’s Ministry of Education in Bangkok yesterday, March 17, to submit a letter objecting to the government’s non-Thai student admissions policy . Tae arrived at around 11am with supporters from the Thai Mai Thon group and handed officials a petition opposing the Ministry of Education announcement on accepting students who do not hold Thai nationality. The group also submitted a list of public signatories, which Tae claimed totalled close to 100,000 names. Speaking

title: Truckers end Laem Chabang rally, will keep pressing for fuel solutions
desc: CHON BURI - Transport operators who gathered near Laem Chabang Port on Wednesday have dispersed after staging a symbolic truck convoy to press the government to urgently tackle soaring fuel prices and domestic shortages.

title: Holy ride? Monks’ speedboat clip goes viral
desc: A viral video showing a group of monks riding a high-speed water attraction at Kham Somboon Beach has sparked widespread debate online, drawing nearly 8 million views.

title: Thailand’s Gawdland wins ‘RuPaul’s Drag Race UK vs The World’
desc: The Thai drag star Gawdland has been crowned the winner of “RuPaul’s Drag Race UK vs The World”, making history as the first Asian to win the international event.

title: 5 monks criticised for enjoying banana boat in Bueng Kan reservoir
desc: 5 monks criticised for enjoying banana boat in Bueng Kan reservoir Legacy Thai monks faced criticism online after a video showed them riding a car-shaped banana boat in the Bueng Khong Long reservoir in Bueng Kan province. A Thai social media influencer posted the clip on his Facebook page, บอยขวัญถุงChannel, showing five monks taking the banana boat ride in the reservoir. The video drew attention across Thai social media and triggered debate about appropriate conduct for monks. The Facebook user later deleted the post and said he did not record the footage himself. He said he had obtained the

title: Oil tax cuts possible if subsidies get too pricey
desc: Officials at the Ministry of Finance are preparing measures to reduce excise taxes on fuel, as well as draft legislation to allow the Oil Fuel Fund to borrow additional funds to stabilise domestic prices.

title: Social Security Fund breaches risk limit
desc: Thailand’s 2.9-trillion-baht Social Security Fund urgently needs to reform governance and diversify its investments, two ​of its executives say, after a recent selloff triggered by the Middle East conflict raised a warning about massive loss risks.

title: XCL Education enters Thailand’s Eastern Economic Corridor with Mooltripakdee partnership
desc: SINGAPORE — XCL Education ("XCL"), one of Southeast Asia's leading K-12 education platforms, has entered a strategic partnership with Mooltripakdee International School (MIS) in Chon Buri province, Thailand — marking a decisive entry into the Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC), one of the region's rapidly developing economic zones.

title: Thailand eyes gains from global shifts
desc: The ongoing conflict involving the United States, Israel and Iran, coupled with other geopolitical tensions, is prompting businesses worldwide to reconsider their production bases, with many seeking relocation to more stable regions, says the Board of Investment (BoI).

title: Jaymart taps 7-Eleven  to expand retail reach
desc: SET-listed Jaymart Group Holdings' handset distribution arm, Jaymart Mobile, is set to expand its network of mini service shops at 7-Eleven locations by 40 branches this year, from two at present.

title: Ten suspects caught en route to Myanmar scam hub
desc: KANCHANABURI - Police have arrested seven Chinese nationals and three Myanmar men at a rented property, where they were reportedly waiting to travel to a scam base in the Myanmar border town of Payathonzu.

title: Thailand warns of fraud-trafficking link
desc: Online fraud is no longer merely a financial crime but a complex web of human trafficking and rights abuses that no country can tackle alone, Thailand has told a global summit.

title: Inspections into oil hoarding ordered
desc: The permanent secretary for energy, Prasert Sinsukprasert, has ordered urgent inspections into suspected oil hoarding, warning that violators will face legal action.

title: Lany returns to Bangkok with 'Soft World Tour'
desc: Paul Klein and Jake Goss of Lany will return to Emsphere's UOB Live to perform their tenth show in Thailand as part of their “Soft World Tour” on Nov 10.

title: White and gold glamour returns to Samui
desc: The legendary "White Party: A Night of White And Gold Glamour" will return to Nikki Beach Koh Samui, Surat Thani, on March 28 from 4pm to 11pm.

title: Bangkok residents urged to dim lights for Earth Hour
desc: Bangkok residents and businesses are being encouraged to switch off non‑essential lights and appliances for one hour on March 28, as part of Earth Hour 2026.

title: TAT in talks to place ‘Amazing Thailand’ on West Ham kit
desc: The Tourism Authority of Thailand (TAT) is in discussions with English Premier League club West Ham United on a potential partnership to promote Thai tourism.

title: Perth and Santa team up for Love You Teacher BL series
desc: Thai BL couple Perth and Santa return in a new romantic comedy Love You Teacher.

title: Necessary drugs, medical supplies ‘enough for three months’
desc: Thailand’s existing stocks of necessary drugs and life-saving medical supplies are enough for three months’ use, the Ministry of Public Health said on Wednesday.

title: Chinese fugitive held in Thailand over 500m baht fraud
desc: KHON KAEN — 18 March 2026, Thai immigration police have arrested a Chinese national wanted for fraud involving about 500 million baht.
